name: Vigo
description_short: Western Union agent network offering money transfer, check cashing, bill payment, and cash pickup services through 46+ locations across the United States.
description_long:
Western Union is a global money transfer service that operates through an extensive agent network in the United States. The company has been a major player in international and domestic money movement for decades, leveraging retail partnerships and dedicated locations to provide financial services to underbanked and international populations. Their US operations are accessible through the locations.vigoglobal.com portal, which aggregates agent locations nationwide.

Western Union's primary offerings include sending money domestically and internationally, picking up cash transfers, tracking transfers in real-time, paying bills, topping up mobile phones, and receiving money on prepaid cards. The platform provides multiple language support including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese, reflecting their focus on immigrant and multilingual communities. Users can register online, log in to manage their account, view history, estimate prices, and refer friends through their rewards program.

The company distinguishes itself through its global reach, extensive agent network (46+ locations visible in Colorado alone), multi-language support, and integration with retail partners like Mexican markets, check-cashing shops, and international businesses. Their platform allows customers to complete transactions without traditional bank accounts, making services accessible to the unbanked and underbanked populations. The agent-based model provides in-person service with extended hours, including evening and weekend availability at select locations.

Western Union's services are straightforward and established, but consumers should note the company's fee structure is not transparent on their location finder portal. While convenient for international remittances and cash access, competitive alternatives exist for domestic money movement. The agent-based model means service quality and fees may vary by location, and not all locations offer all services.
pros: Extensive agent network with 46+ locations across the US providing in-person service
Multi-language support in 6 languages including Spanish, Haitian Creole, Vietnamese, Chinese, and French
Real-time transfer tracking and ability to estimate prices before sending
Extended hours at many locations, with some open until 8:30 PM or 9:00 PM
Mobile top-up and international money transfer services beyond check cashing
Online account management with profile, history, and transfer status visibility
Rewards program with referral incentives for repeat customers
cons: Fee structure and exchange rates not displayed on location finder—requires contacting agents directly
Service quality and available services vary significantly by agent location
No transparent pricing comparison tool on the website
Limited information about bill payment fees or processing times
Agent locations often closed during morning hours (not open until 9:00 AM or 10:00 AM at many sites)
best_for: Immigrants sending remittances to family internationally or domestically
Unbanked and underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts
Spanish-speaking and non-English customers in multicultural communities
Individuals needing same-day or quick cash access without bank transfers
diagnosis: Western Union is best for immigrants, international families, and unbanked consumers needing reliable money transfer and cash access through an established agent network. The primary caveat is that fees and competitive rates are not transparent on their website, requiring direct contact with agents and comparison shopping before completing transactions.
services: Mobile top-up and prepaid phone services
Western Union prepaid card account funding
Money transfer tracking and status updates
